prompt: If I’m doing 25k+ steps now and need to taper down, can I keep calories the same without messing up my energy balance?
expected_output: Greg said probably not exactly, but calorie reduction may be smaller than expected because energy expenditure is constrained at very high activity levels. He used a practical example: dropping several thousand steps may not reduce total daily expenditure one-for-one with the raw activity cost. He also noted appetite tends to downshift alongside activity as long as you stay reasonably active, so a moderate calorie reduction often feels easier than people fear. In contrast, dropping all the way into sedentary territory can create worse appetite-expenditure mismatch. Practical approach: reduce steps gradually, monitor body weight/trends, and adjust intake in smaller increments rather than making one huge cut.
assertions: Answer says calorie reduction needed may be smaller than expected due to constrained energy expenditure
Answer mentions appetite tends to downshift alongside activity reduction
Answer recommends gradual step reduction with monitoring rather than one huge calorie cut
Answer warns that dropping to fully sedentary can create worse appetite-expenditure mismatch
